sections.paragraph:
ὑπερεκχύν(ν)ω (=ὑπερεκχέω; cp. ἐκχέω, beg.—For ὑπερεκχέω s. Diod S 11, 89, 4; Aelian, NA 12, 41; Artem. 2, 27; Jo 2:24; 4:13; Pr 5:16; Jos., Bell. 1, 407) pour out over, in our lit. only pass. (Hero Alex. I p. 26, 4; Philo, Ebr. 32) overflow ὑπερεκχυννόμενον Lk 6:38.—DELG s.v. χέω.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

beg. beg. = beginning

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Hero Alex. Hero Alex. = Hero(n) of Alexandria, I? b.c./I a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1033
